steps.title: Join the festival clauses
steps.content: The sentence first says Novak's work was featured at a photography festival in Italy, with for example parenthetically clarifying that location. A second independent clause then says the photographs captivated audiences, so a semicolon is needed after the parenthetical.

steps.title: Reject misplaced punctuation
steps.content: Choice B uses only a comma between independent clauses. Choice C puts the semicolon before for example, separating the parenthetical from the location it clarifies. Choice D provides no punctuation before the second clause. The answer is A.
